title: Fixing Git Tag Name Validation for Long Names

statement.markdown: Write a function that validates a Git tag name. A valid tag name should be in the format of `v<version>`, where `<version>` is a string of digits and can have a maximum length of 100. The function should return `true` if the tag name is valid and `false` otherwise. The function should handle both short and long tag names correctly.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the Git tag name
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the tag name is valid
statement.notes: The function should not consider the leading `v` as a version number if it exceeds the maximum length of 100.

constraints.description: Maximum length of the tag name
Maximum length of the version number

examples.explanation: Valid tag name with short version number
Invalid tag name with long version number
Valid tag name with long version number
Invalid tag name without version number
Valid tag name with multiple dots in version number

hints.text: First hint text: Check if the tag name starts with 'v' and has a version number after it. Then check the length of the version number.
Second hint text: The function should handle both short and long tag names correctly. It should also handle tag names with multiple dots in the version number.

editorial.title: Editorial title: Fixing Git Tag Name Validation for Long Names
editorial.intuition: Explanation of the correct approach: The function should first check if the tag name starts with 'v'. If it does, it should then check the length of the version number. If the length of the version number is within the allowed range, the function should return true. Otherwise, it should return false.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach summary: The function can iterate over each character in the tag name and check if it is a digit. If it is, it can add the digit to the version number. If the version number exceeds the maximum length, the function can return false. After checking all characters in the tag name, the function can return true if the version number is within the allowed range.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution details: The function can use a regular expression to match the tag name against the allowed pattern. If the pattern matches, the function can return true. Otherwise, it can return false.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof: The function should return true if the tag name is in the format of 'v<version>' where '<version>' is a string of digits and has a maximum length of 100. It should return false otherwise.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: Given the input 'v1.0', the function should return true. Given the input 'v999999999999999999999999999999999', the function should return false.
commonMistakes: Mistake 1: Not handling long tag names correctly
Mistake 2: Not handling tag names with multiple dots in the version number
